title: Provenance-manifest format (snapshot + rule + hash + TTL + jurisdiction)
acceptanceCriteria: A JSON Schema for the manifest format is defined with all required fields: item URL, raw API snapshot, rights field value, determination, rule ID, retrieval timestamp (ISO 8601), content hash (algorithm + hex), determination TTL, and jurisdiction.
The format is documented in a human-readable spec explaining what each field contains and why.
The TTL field is defined with a proposed default of 180 days and the rationale recorded.
The manifest is frozen: it records what was true at clear-time and does not reference live LoC metadata.
The maintainer has reviewed and approved the format before any data tasks use it.
objective: Define the provenance-manifest format (JSON Schema plus human-readable spec) recording: the raw LoC API response snapshot, the resolved rights determination, the rule that produced it, the LoC item URL, a retrieval timestamp, a content hash, a determination TTL, and the jurisdiction.
output: A committed provenance-manifest JSON Schema plus a human-readable format spec, approved by the maintainer.
context: Every cleared item must carry a frozen provenance manifest so that every rights clear is reproducible and auditable even if LoC live metadata later changes. The manifest records what the metadata said at clear-time, which rule cleared it, and when the determination expires (TTL). Without it, the rights audit cannot function. Depends on rights-001. See PLAN.md (Solution approach section 5, Key data artifacts).
